availability.summary: A live POST probe on 2026-05-12 returned HTTP 200 JSON with the documented aggregate rollup fields, confirming the endpoint is currently callable.

sources.title: Staged docs for Award Funding Rollup
Award funding rollup view and validator
Derived MCP request coverage check for funding rollup
Live baseline probe for award funding rollup

parameters.description: Award identifier for the rollup lookup. Source code accepts either a generated award id string such as CONT_AWD_... or an integer internal award id.

documented.notes: Docs explicitly list award_id as the required request attribute.

observed.notes: A generated award id string succeeded in a live POST. Current-profile and source evidence indicate integer internal award ids are also accepted.

constraints: Required JSON body field.
Source code validates either an integer internal award id or a text generated award id.
If award_id is an integer the lookup uses the internal award_id column; otherwise it uses generated_unique_award_id.

parameters.description: Legacy pagination field shown in the docs sample body, but the live implementation ignores it and still returns one aggregate object.

documented.notes: Shown only in the sample request body, not in the formal request attributes.

observed.notes: Current-profile and source evidence indicate the view reads only award_id.

constraints: Documented example field only.

templates.name: basic-award-funding-rollup
templates.description: Primary safe request template for this aggregate endpoint: send only award_id.

response.shapeSummary: Single aggregate JSON object with one monetary total and three distinct-count fields; there is no results array or page metadata.

fields.description: Sum of transaction_obligated_amount across revealed financial_accounts_by_awards rows associated with the requested award.

observed.notes: The live sample returned a positive value; current-profile evidence indicates some awards can yield negative totals.

constraints: This is an aggregate total, not a list of transaction rows.
Source SQL limits the contributing rows to revealed submission windows.

fields.description: Count of distinct top-tier awarding agencies represented in the revealed File C funding records for the award.
constraints: This is a distinct-agency count, not a row count.
Source SQL counts distinct awarding top-tier agency ids.

fields.description: Count of distinct top-tier funding agencies represented in the revealed File C funding records for the award.
constraints: This is a distinct-agency count, not a row count.
Source SQL counts distinct funding top-tier agency ids.

fields.description: Count of distinct federal accounts represented in the revealed File C funding records for the award.
constraints: This is a distinct-account count, not a row count.
Source SQL counts distinct treasury appropriation agency_id-main_account_code combinations.

pagination.notes: Despite the docs sample body showing page, sort, order, and limit, the endpoint returns one aggregate object and no pagination metadata.

notes: The current raw MCP profile only exposes award_id.
The omitted fields come from the staged docs sample body and matter mainly because callers need to understand they are contradicted legacy fields rather than active controls.

contradictions.statement: Documentation shows page, sort, order, and limit in the sample request body, but source code and prior profile evidence indicate the view only reads award_id and always returns a single aggregate object.
contradictions.impact: Callers should not expect pagination or sorting behavior from those legacy fields.

quirks.statement: If award_id is an integer, the view looks up the internal award_id column; otherwise it looks up generated_unique_award_id.
quirks.impact: Generated award id strings are the common path, but integer surrogate ids remain part of the accepted validator contract.

quirks.statement: Unknown or unmatched award identifiers are reported by the current raw profile as HTTP 200 responses with zero totals rather than errors.
quirks.impact: A zeroed response is not sufficient proof that the supplied award_id was semantically correct.

gaps.statement: This bundle has not freshly re-probed whitespace-only award_id handling or content-type enforcement; those behaviors are carried from source/profile evidence rather than a new live probe.

risks.statement: An incorrect award_id can silently yield zero totals instead of an error.
risks.impact: Validate or source the award identifier from a trusted upstream step before interpreting a zero rollup as real data.

risks.statement: total_transaction_obligated_amount is a sum of underlying transaction obligations and may be negative for some awards.
risks.impact: Downstream analytics should not assume the rollup total is always positive.

risks.statement: The count fields are distinct counts over revealed File C funding records, not counts of funding rows or transactions.
risks.impact: Do not interpret awarding_agency_count, funding_agency_count, or federal_account_count as record counts.
